workload_target: future cyclic-position or adapter experiment; no workload run in this fixture
structural_invariant: phase-bank or block-cyclic structure is recorded separately from quality
proof_or_receipt_boundary: external proof-linked source note only; no local Circle build

structural_claims: phase-bank or block-cyclic structure is recordable

alias_diagnostics: residue and winding must be preserved when needed
parameter_accounting: parameter count is a separate field from quality and runtime
hardware_kernel_notes: hardware-friendly sizes and kernel costs remain residuals
hardware_refusal_path: block adoption or narrow scope if deployment kernels, layouts, or precision assumptions are unavailable
baseline_symmetry_policy: candidate and baselines must share workload, metric, data condition, hardware notes, and fallback policy
consumer_policy: May support structural diagnostics only; quality, runtime, memory, or canary routing require measured tradeoffs.
adoption_decision_rationale: fixture has structural-source context but no baseline-symmetric workload evidence
